 {"id": "bd-3f6f", "title": "CI: comprehensive pipeline integration", "description": "Comprehensive CI pipeline for all quality gates.

Goal: Automated CI that validates all FrankenLibC quality gates.

CI Jobs:

1. Build Matrix
   - cargo build --release (all targets)
   - cargo build --release -p frankenlibc-abi
   - cargo build --features=standalone (L2+)

2. Quality Gates
   - cargo fmt --check
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - cargo test --all-targets

3. Conformance Suite
   - scripts/ci.sh (basic gate)
   - scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh
   - scripts/snapshot_gate.sh

4. LD_PRELOAD Smoke
   - scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh (strict)
   - FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh

5. Performance Gates
   - cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench
   - Strict <20ns validation
   - Hardened <200ns validation
   - No regressions vs baseline

6. CVE Arena
   - All CVE triggers pass in hardened mode
   - Metrics capture for evidence

7. Fuzzing (nightly)
   - 1M iterations per fuzz target
   - Crash reporting
   - Coverage report

8. Multi-arch (when available)
   - aarch64 cross-compile
   - QEMU test run

Artifacts:
- Build logs
- Test results (JUnit XML)
- Coverage reports
- Benchmark results
- Fuzzing coverage

Success Criteria:
- All gates pass on every PR
- Performance baselines maintained
- Coverage reports generated
- Nightly fuzzing runs

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CalmBadger", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:04:05.833424316Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-16T16:40:10.377640263Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-16T16:40:10.377621768Z", "close_reason": "Implemented comprehensive CI pipeline integration: core/extended/release/fuzz/multi-arch lanes, explicit fuzz execution budgets, artifact persistence, and validated command viability with rch offload.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "infra"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-3f6f", "depends_on_id": "bd-25n", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3f6f", "depends_on_id": "bd-2ez", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 972, "issue_id": "bd-3f6f", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16): started `bd-3f6f` and landed CI workflow bootstrap.

Notes:

 {"id": "bd-3fb", "title": "EPIC: Extreme Optimization Loop (baselines + profiles + goldens)", "description": "Enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop for allocator + membrane:
BASELINE -> PROFILE -> PROVE -> IMPLEMENT (one lever) -> VERIFY -> REPEAT.

Deliverables:
- Baseline suite (hyperfine + criterion).
- Flamegraph/profiler playbook.
- Golden outputs + isomorphism proof template wired into PR process.

Acceptance:
- We can prove behavior unchanged after optimizations via goldens/checksums.
- Perf regressions are detected quickly and reproducibly.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:36:07.189454921Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T18:51:02.023072672Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T18:51:02.023028389Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "perf"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-144", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-1ik", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-22p", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-26o", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-2bd", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-2r0", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-2wp", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3fb", "depends_on_id": "bd-f7r", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-3g4p", "title": "Support matrix: automated maintenance", "description": "Automated support matrix maintenance.

Goal: Keep support_matrix.json synchronized with code reality.

Automation Components:

1. Symbol Discovery
   - Parse exported symbols from libfrankenlibc_abi.so
   - Compare against support_matrix.json
   - Report new/removed symbols

2. Status Validation
   - For Implemented: verify no todo!/unimplemented!
   - For RawSyscall: verify syscall veneer usage
   - For GlibcCallThrough: verify glibc call
   - For Stub: verify deterministic fallback

3. Conformance Linkage
   - Each symbol links to fixture files
   - Each symbol links to test commands
   - Coverage report generation

4. Drift Detection
   - CI gate fails on drift
   - PR comments with drift summary
   - Automated fix suggestions

5. Report Generation
   - reality_report.v1.json from support_matrix
   - Feature parity reports
   - Coverage dashboards

CI Integration:
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on drift
- Generate coverage reports

Success Criteria:
- Zero manual maintenance of support_matrix
- Drift detected within one PR
- Reports always current

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id": "bd-3h1u", "title": "EPIC: Methodology and Infrastructure", "description": "Infrastructure and methodology beads that support the overall FrankenLibC development process. Covers: baseline performance capture for all 250 symbols, evidence ledger extensions for safety decisions, property-based testing framework, CI artifact gates, and decision contracts for strict/hardened mode. These are the foundational capabilities that make all other epics possible — without baseline measurements, you cannot prove optimization. Without evidence ledger, you cannot audit safety decisions. Without property-based testing, you cannot find edge cases. Without CI gates, you cannot prevent regressions.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Methodology and infrastructure foundations from CMM/CMMI Level 3+ practices. Measurement-driven development from Basili & Weiss (1984) GQM paradigm.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Baseline infrastructure: criterion.rs benchmark suite in benches/.
- Evidence ledger: src/evidence/ledger.rs with append-only storage.
- Property-based testing: proptest strategies in tests/properties/.
- CI gates: .github/workflows/gates.yml orchestrating all quality checks.
- Decision contract: src/tsm/decision_contract.rs as const state machine.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
1. Baseline capture operational for all 250 symbols.
2. Evidence ledger extended with galaxy-brain card format.
3. Property-based tests covering all 6 ABI families.
4. CI artifact gates enforced on all PRs.
5. Decision contract implemented and verified.

### Mandatory Verification Gate

This epic encodes the non-negotiable loop: **baseline -> profile -> prove -> implement -> verify -> repeat**.

### What We Are Protecting
runtime_math runs at the ABI boundary of libc call paths. Any accidental overhead shows up everywhere.

Budgets (from AGENTS.md):
- strict overhead target: <20ns/call (fast exits)
- hardened overhead target: <200ns/call (bounded heavy path)

### What Counts As “Hot Path”
- `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` on the strict path
- per-call evidence recording (if enabled)
- pointer-validation stage ordering / cached biases

### Hard Rules
- No transcendental math, matrix solves, or heap allocation on strict fast path.
- Any stochastic element must be deterministic from seed (replayable).
- Any “expensive” kernel must be cadence-driven (epoch update) not per call.

### Measurement Artifacts Required
- Criterion microbench for `decide` / `observe` (ns/op + percentiles)
- Profile recipe (flamegraph/perf) pinned to a reproducible command
- Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate
- Isomorphism proof template for policy changes (behavior invariants)

### Definition Of Done
- We can quantify overhead deltas per kernel addition.
- We can detect regressions in CI before merging.
- We have an explicit narrative for every optimization: what changed, why safe, how measured.", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:52:00Z"}, {"id": 1056, "issue_id": "bd-73r", "author": "CobaltCompass", "text": "EPIC CLOSED by CobaltCompass.  {"id": "bd-ahjd", "title": "bd-2ry subtask: Deterministic fixture pack for nested/edge non-local jump scenarios", "description": "Background:

- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.

- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id": "bd-am9u", "title": "EPIC: Dynamic System Constants — Replace hard-coded values with runtime queries", "description": "FrankenLibC currently hard-codes several system-dependent constants (page size, stack limits, group counts, FD limits) that should be queried at runtime for cross-architecture correctness.

BACKGROUND: While FrankenLibC currently targets x86_64 Linux, these hard-coded values create latent bugs that will surface when porting to ARM64 (16KB/64KB pages), RISC-V, or systems with non-standard configurations. Even on x86_64, some values (like supplementary group count) can differ from defaults.

SCOPE: This epic covers all hard-coded constants identified in the comprehensive audit across malloc_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, glibc_internal_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, pthread_abi.rs, and process_abi.rs.

ALREADY DONE: sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE) now queries AT_PAGESZ from /proc/self/auxv at runtime (cached). The mmap_alloc bootstrap path intentionally keeps 4096 as the minimum alignment (documented).

RATIONALE: AGENTS.md says "We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT" — hard-coded system constants are textbook tech debt.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "assignee": "PlumCove", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:03:22.060285167Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:03:59.322247279Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:03:59.321965265Z", "close_reason": "Delivered: code + artifacts present.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
